hero.title: Free Online
hero.description: Resize JPG/JPEG images with quality control. Perfect for photos and web images. Adjust dimensions while maintaining image quality.

dropzone.orClick: or click to browse JPG/JPEG files only

custom.maintainAspect: Maintain aspect ratio (optional for exact sizing)
custom.tipText: Enable aspect ratio to prevent image distortion

advanced.title: Advanced Settings
advanced.stripMetadata: Strip metadata (EXIF, ICC) for smaller files

notices.startResizeHint: Click "Start Resize" to resize your images.
notices.settingsChangedHint: Settings have changed. Click "Start Resize" to apply new settings to your images.

privacy.title: Data & Privacy
privacy.p1: All image resizing happens locally in your browser using HTML5 Canvas. Your images never leave your device.
privacy.p2: No uploads to any server. No storage. No tracking of your image content.
privacy.p3: Your privacy is 100% protected - everything runs client-side on your machine.
privacy.p4: Close the tab anytime. Your original images remain untouched.
privacy.notice: All processing happens in your browser, no uploads.

howTo.title: How To Resize JPG Image Online
howTo.step1Desc: Drop your JPG/JPEG photos into the upload area. Everything is processed locally in your browser.
howTo.step2Desc: Set exact pixel sizes, scale by percentage, or pick a preset. Use constraints to keep photos looking natural.
howTo.step3Desc: Download resized JPGs individually or as a ZIP. Adjust quality when exporting to balance size and clarity.
howTo.tipText: JPG is ideal for photos. Use the quality slider to balance file size and image quality. Fill mode is best for exact dimensions; use aspect ratio to avoid distortion.

why.title: Why Resize JPG?
why.p1: Resizing JPGs helps optimize photos for the web while maintaining quality. Properly sized images improve load time and Core Web Vitals, and look better on social platforms.
why.tipText: Export at around 80–90% quality for a great balance of size and clarity.

platform.title: Perfect Size for Every Platform
platform.card1Desc: Resize and fine-tune output quality so images stay sharp without becoming unnecessarily large.
platform.card2Desc: Serving correctly sized photos improves performance and helps your pages rank better.
platform.card3Desc: Resize many JPGs at once directly in the browser with no uploads.

qualityGuide.title: Quality vs Size Guide
qualityGuide.introPrefix: Different output formats produce different file sizes.
qualityGuide.introMiddle1: offers the smallest files, followed by
qualityGuide.introSuffix: Adjust the quality slider to see how it affects file size.
qualityGuide.recommendedDesc: Web images • Social media • Best balance

items.question: Will resizing reduce JPG quality?
items.answer: Resizing changes the pixel dimensions. If you keep output as JPEG, you can control compression with the quality slider to keep images looking sharp.

items.question: What quality should I use for web images?
items.answer: For most web use, 80–90% quality is a strong balance between file size and visual clarity.

items.question: Can I upscale a small JPG to a larger size?
items.answer: Yes, but upscaling can make images look softer. Enable Prevent upscaling if you want to avoid enlarging beyond the original size.

items.question: Is this JPG resizer fully private?
items.answer: Yes. Everything runs locally in your browser and your images are never uploaded to a server.
